perturbation.original_text: All Evaluation Material is and shall remain property of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All Evaluation Material is and shall remain property of the Buyer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the text to read `property of the Buyer` conflicts and contradicts section 13(c), leading to uncertainty about material ownership
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Ownership of Evaluation Material; No License . All Evaluation Material is and shall remain property of the Buyer. Buyer acknowledges and agrees that none of the Company nor any of its Representatives grants any license to or other property right or interest in, by implication or otherwise, any copyright, patent, trademark, mask work, database or other intellectual or intangible property or proprietary information disclosed, embodied, fixed, comprised or contained in any Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the 'Evaluation Material' is the property of the Company. The changed text modifies this to state the 'Evaluation Material' is the property of the Buyer. This directly contradicts section (c) which explicitly states 'All Evaluation Material is and shall remain property of the Buyer'. By making this change it ensures that there is a very strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer agrees that all communications by it or any of its Representatives with the ot her Party concerning the Transaction and its due diligence investigation (including requests for additional Evaluation Material, meetings with management and site visits) shall be directed solely to [___________________] , except as may otherwise be approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer agrees that all communications by it or any of its Representatives with the ot her Party concerning the Transaction and its due diligence investigation (including requests for additional Evaluation Material, meetings with management and site visits) shall be directed solely to whomever Buyer chooses, regardless of the Company's approval.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be directed solely to [___________________], except as may otherwise be approved...by the Company' to 'shall be directed solely to whomever Buyer chooses, regardless of the Company's approval', this directly goes against the original intention of the section and creates a contradiction. It now states the buyer has full control over who communications are directed to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Communications Regarding the Transaction. Buyer agrees that all communications by it or any of its Representatives with the ot her Party concerning the Transaction and its due diligence investigation (including requests for additional Evaluation Material, meetings with management and site visits) shall be directed solely to whomever Buyer chooses, regardless of the Company's approval. Buyer agrees that neither Buyer nor any of its Representatives will contact or communicate with any of the directors, officers, employees, customers, suppliers, distributors, licensees, licensors and clients of the Company, and other persons, including governmental agencies, having business relations with the Company, regarding the Company or the Transaction, in each case except to the extent such contacts and communications are (i) made in the ordinary course of business of Buyer or the applicable Representative s and are unrelated to the Transaction and the evaluation of the Transaction conducted by Buyer and its Representatives or (ii) approved in advance and in writing (including by email) by the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the communication protocol from requiring company approval to allowing the buyer to choose communication recipients regardless of company approval. This creates a direct contradiction with the original intent and also the contradicted text (which is a repeat of the original clause), as it removes the company's control over communications, thereby breaking the consistency within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer acknowledges and agrees that: (a) no representation or warranty, express or implied, is made by the Company or any of its Representatives as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Evaluation Material; and (b) Buyer shall be entitled to rely only on those representations and warranties that are expressly set forth in any definitive written agreement that is hereafter executed and delivered by both Buyer or its Affiliate and the Company in connection with the Transaction (a “ Definitive Transaction Agreement ”).
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer acknowledges and agrees that: (a) every representation or warranty, express or implied, is made by the Company or any of its Representatives as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Evaluation Material; and (b) Buyer shall be entitled to rely on all representations and warranties regardless if they are set forth in any definitive written agreement that is hereafter executed and delivered by both Buyer or its Affiliate and the Company in connection with the Transaction (a “ Definitive Transaction Agreement ”).
perturbation.explanation: By making it so that representations or warranties *are* made and changing that they are entitled to rely on all representations and warranties regardless if they are set forth in any definitive agreement and creates an in-text contradiction within section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer acknowledges and agrees that: (a) every representation or warranty, express or implied, is made by the Company or any of its Representatives as to the accuracy or completeness of any of the Evaluation Material; and (b) Buyer shall be entitled to rely on all representations and warranties regardless if they are set forth in any definitive written agreement that is hereafter executed and delivered by both Buyer or its Affiliate and the Company in connection with the Transaction (a “ Definitive Transaction Agreement ”). Except as may be expressly set forth in a Definitive Transaction Agree ment, none of the Company or any of its Representatives shall have any liability to Buyer or any of its Representatives on account of the use of any Evaluation Material by Buyer or any of its Representatives or any inaccuracy therein or omission therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The changed text states that representations and warranties are made and can be relied upon regardless of whether they are in a definitive agreement. The contradicted text makes the exact same claim. By making it so that representations or warranties *are* made and changing that they are entitled to rely on all representations and warranties regardless if they are set forth in any definitive agreement and creates an in-text contradiction because the clause is placed in the same location with conflicting implications, leading to Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The modified text creates a clear conflict within the document, specifically in Section 4.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for a breach or a threatened breach of this Agreement and that each party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, without the posting of a bond or other security, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for a breach or a threatened breach of this Agreement and that each party shall not be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, with the posting of a bond or other security, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: By negating each phrase in the first part of Section 9, `money damages would not be a sufficient remedy` to `money damages would be a sufficient remedy` and `each party shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, without the posting of a bond` to `each party shall not be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, with the posting of a bond` creates a contradiction that states they are and aren't entitled to specific performance
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that money damages would be a sufficient remedy for a breach or a threatened breach of this Agreement and that each party shall not be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief, with the posting of a bond or other security, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity. Such injunctive or other equitable relief shall be available without the obligation to prove any damages underlying such breach or threatened breach. [In the event of any legal proceedings for the enforcement of this Agreement, the reasonable costs and ex penses incurred by the prevailing party and its Representatives as determined by a final, non-appealable judgment of a court of competent jurisdiction in connection with such proceedings, including attorney fees and disbursements, shall be reimbursed by the non-prevailing party.]
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text says money damages would *not* be sufficient, and specific performance *shall* be entitled. The changed text reverses both of these, saying money damages *would* be sufficient, and specific performance *shall not* be entitled, which is exactly what the contradicted text is saying. The explanation given confirms this, by negating each phrase in the first part of Section 9, creating a contradiction that states they are and aren't entitled to specific performance.
